skill.name: doctoral-ti-news-cvlac
skill.description: Finds recent information technology news and links it to verifiable academic evidence, with Colombia-focused enrichment through CvLAC, GrupLAC, Publindex, OpenAlex, and Crossref.
skill.summary: Connect recent TI news with verified academic evidence and Colombian researcher metadata.
skill.author: Jhusef Alfonso López Parra

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found these are Markdown examples, schema field names, URLs, and academic metadata text rather than executable code. The only confirmed risk factor is intentional network use for public academic and official Colombian research sources, with clear guidance to avoid credentials, sensitive identity data, and mass scraping.

low_findings.title: Intentional Network Access to Public Research Sources
low_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to use WebSearch, WebFetch, OpenAlex, Crossref, and official MinCiencias domains. This is required for the research workflow and is constrained to public sources, HTTPS, and no credentials or cookies.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is explicit and central to the skill purpose. The same files include safety limits that reduce exfiltration and privacy risk.

low_findings.title: External Command Static Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported command execution patterns occur inside Markdown tables, fenced examples, output examples, or inline field names. No executable script or shell invocation path was found in the reviewed skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Representative flagged locations are Markdown documentation or JSON examples, not runnable source. No evidence found of shell execution or user-controlled command construction.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections map to schema metadata, DOI-like text, JSON field names, academic metrics, or Markdown examples. No hashing, encryption, signature, or password-storage implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited contexts define output schema and illustrative metadata. They do not perform cryptographic operations or recommend weak algorithms.

content.value_statement: Researchers need current technology news linked to credible scientific evidence. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to connect news, papers, CvLAC profiles, GrupLAC records, and Publindex data with traceable sources.
actual_capabilities: Guides searches for recent TI news and links each item to papers or verified academic metadata.
Enriches Colombian authors with CvLAC profile checks, GrupLAC references, and confidence notes.
Checks DOI, venue, publication status, h-index source, and open access metadata through public sources.
Adds Publindex context for Colombian journals when a verifiable source is available.
Produces Spanish or English research summaries with traceable text and structured output guidance.
Flags ambiguous authors, missing papers, retractions, and low-confidence evidence instead of inventing data.
limitations: CvLAC has no public API, so profile checks depend on web availability and manual disambiguation.
The skill does not guarantee complete coverage of all Colombian researchers or journals.
It should not be used for mass scraping or extraction of identity document numbers.
Metrics such as h-index and quartile can differ by source and date.

use_cases.title: Prepare Doctoral Research Scans
use_cases.description: Find recent TI news and connect it to papers, doctoral questions, and Colombia-specific research gaps.

use_cases.title: Validate Colombian Author Context
use_cases.description: Check whether Colombian authors have matching CvLAC profiles, GrupLAC links, and publication evidence.

use_cases.title: Track Journal and Venue Signals
use_cases.description: Compare international venue metrics with Publindex status for Colombian journals and research outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Basic News Scan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a short Colombian TI scan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find recent Colombian TI news from the last 90 days about artificial intelligence in education. Link each news item to any verifiable paper, DOI, or official academic source. Return a concise Spanish summary.

prompt_templates.title: CvLAC Author Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request focused on Colombian researcher verification.
prompt_templates.prompt: For this DOI, identify Colombian authors, verify possible CvLAC profiles, and report affiliation, confidence level, GrupLAC links, and whether the publication appears in the profile. Do not infer missing data.

prompt_templates.title: Publindex Venue Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review recent papers about cybersecurity in Colombian critical infrastructure. For Colombian journals, verify Publindex category. For international journals, provide SJR quartile or mark it as not confirmed.

prompt_templates.title: Full Doctoral Surveillance Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a complete research intelligence workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a deep doctoral surveillance brief on edge computing and IoT in LATAM. Prioritize Colombia, enrich Colombian authors through CvLAC when possible, include methodology risks, research gaps, and traceable source notes.

output_examples.input: Recent Colombian news about generative AI in public services.
output_examples.output: A Spanish executive summary with ranked findings, linked papers, DOI status, author affiliations, CvLAC confidence notes, and doctoral research questions.

output_examples.input: A DOI for a paper with possible Colombian coauthors.
output_examples.output: An author-focused verification report that lists Colombian affiliations, possible CvLAC profile matches, GrupLAC context, and reasons for any low-confidence match.

output_examples.input: A Colombian journal title and a topic in software engineering.
output_examples.output: A venue note that separates Publindex status, international metrics, missing evidence, and recommended follow-up checks.

best_practices: Use exact author names, affiliations, DOI values, and institutional names when available.
Mark missing or ambiguous CvLAC, Publindex, and metric data as unconfirmed instead of guessing.
Limit CvLAC checks to relevant Colombian authors and document every source used.
anti_patterns: Do not assign a CvLAC profile using only a common surname.
Do not report identity document numbers, private data, cookies, or credentials.
Do not treat media claims as scientific evidence without a linked paper or official source.

faq.question: Does this skill access CvLAC through an API?
faq.answer: No. It guides targeted web checks because CvLAC does not provide a public API for this workflow.

faq.question: Can it verify every Colombian author automatically?
faq.answer: No. Homonyms and incomplete profiles require confidence notes and sometimes manual review.

faq.question: Does Publindex replace international journal metrics?
faq.answer: No. Publindex is Colombian context. SJR, OpenAlex, and other sources provide complementary international signals.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with personal data?
faq.answer: Use only public academic metadata. The skill explicitly avoids identity document numbers and sensitive personal data.

faq.question: What happens when evidence is missing?
faq.answer: The skill instructs the agent to mark data as null, no confirmado, or no disponible and explain the gap.
